description: Complete guide to C++ data types: fundamental types, modifiers, strings, bool, type casting, sizeof, and constants. Includes many examples.

sections.title: Fundamental Data Types
sections.definition: C++ provides several built-in data types to store different kinds of values.
sections.output:
int: 100
float: 3.14159
double: 2.71828
char: X
bool (true=1): 1

sections.title: Type Modifiers
sections.definition: Modifiers change the size or sign of basic types: signed, unsigned, short, long.

sections.title: The string Type
sections.definition: std::string (from <string>) stores sequences of characters. It's more convenient than C-style char arrays.

sections.title: sizeof Operator
sections.definition: sizeof returns the size in bytes of a type or variable. Useful for understanding memory usage.
sections.output:
Size of char: 1 byte(s)
Size of int: 4 bytes
Size of float: 4 bytes
Size of double: 8 bytes
Size of bool: 1 byte(s)
Size of array of 10 ints: 40 bytes
Number of elements: 10

sections.title: Constants and Literals
sections.definition: Constants are fixed values. Use const keyword or #define preprocessor. Literals are explicit values like 42, 3.14, 'A'.

sections.title: Type Casting (Conversion)
sections.definition: Convert one data type to another using static_cast, or C-style casts. Implicit conversion happens automatically.
sections.output:
Implicit: int from double = 3
Implicit: double from int = 5
C-style cast: 3
static_cast: 9
ASCII 65 is char: A
Overflow example: 16960

sections.title: auto Keyword (Type Deduction)
sections.definition: auto lets the compiler deduce the type of a variable from its initializer (C++11).
